sourceShortSummaries: Kaleho dines with Trok in Hamri when Orden begs them to help find his missing daughter Hiyasa.
sourceLongSummaries: On the beach at Hamri, Kaleho joins Trok for an evening meal and the group recalls the prior tower adventure, including the older elemental mysteries tied to Airion the Mistspeaker. Orden then arrives in distress and explains that his daughter Hiyasa recently returned after a decade away, spoke of ancient treasure upriver, and vanished eight days ago. Trok and Kaleho agree to look into it and decide to seek out Ryu the next morning.

sourceEntries.title: Orden's Plea
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The opening dinner scene at Trok's shack establishes the missing-daughter hook and ends with the plan to seek Ryu at breakfast.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Kaleho dines with Trok in Hamri when Orden begs them to help find his missing daughter Hiyasa.
sourceEntries.longSummary: On the beach at Hamri, Kaleho joins Trok for an evening meal and the group recalls the prior tower adventure, including the older elemental mysteries tied to Airion the Mistspeaker. Orden then arrives in distress and explains that his daughter Hiyasa recently returned after a decade away, spoke of ancient treasure upriver, and vanished eight days ago. Trok and Kaleho agree to look into it and decide to seek out Ryu the next morning.

locationHints.context: Trok's shack on the beach below the towers
locationHints.notes: The opening scene takes place at Trok's home on the shore.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: A beach-dweller in Hamri who asks the party to find Hiyasa.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Recently returned to Hamri after ten years away and then vanished while seeking treasure upriver.

npcDetails.name: Airion the Mistspeaker
npcDetails.notes: Referenced in the recap of the previous tower adventure.

sourceShortSummaries: The next morning the party recruits Ryu and Nerissa, then questions Abelard at the Leviathan before leaving Hamri.
sourceLongSummaries: At breakfast the next morning, Trok and Kaleho bring Orden's problem to Ryu and explain the plan to search upriver. Nerissa, an older scholar living nearby, introduces herself over tea and biscuits and volunteers to join once she hears about the possibility of ancient ruins. The group then stops at the Leviathan, where Abelard confirms that a woman matching Hiyasa's description went up the Mirmir after boasting of treasure in an ancient tomb.

sourceEntries.title: Breakfast and Rumors
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The next morning's pre-adventure scenes recruit Ryu and Nerissa, gather the Abelard river lead at the Leviathan, and end as the party prepares to leave Hamri.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The next morning the party recruits Ryu and Nerissa, then questions Abelard at the Leviathan before leaving Hamri.
sourceEntries.longSummary: At breakfast the next morning, Trok and Kaleho bring Orden's problem to Ryu and explain the plan to search upriver. Nerissa, an older scholar living nearby, introduces herself over tea and biscuits and volunteers to join once she hears about the possibility of ancient ruins. The group then stops at the Leviathan, where Abelard confirms that a woman matching Hiyasa's description went up the Mirmir after boasting of treasure in an ancient tomb.

locationHints.context: Ryu's tower, the Leviathan Inn, and final preparations in town
locationHints.notes: The beat combines the morning recruitment and rumor-gathering scenes before the party leaves Hamri.

npcDetails.name: Abelard
npcDetails.notes: Confirms that Hiyasa headed upriver after speaking of an ancient tomb.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Abelard recognizes her description and route.

sourceShortSummaries: Ryu leads the party up the Mirmir and through the forest to Yerkir-khor's buried monument.
sourceLongSummaries: Leaving Hamri, the party climbs above the waterfall and follows the Mirmir inland through autumn forest, relying on Ryu's tracking and Blu's scouting to stay on Hiyasa's trail. They make camp after dark, resume the search the next day, find Hiyasa's abandoned campsite, and follow the cut trail farther from the river. After a day and a half of pursuit, they reach a massive stone flower-like monument inscribed in Terran as the resting place of Yerkir-khor the Undaunted, inspect the ropes and open trap door, and stop just before anyone begins the descent.

sourceEntries.title: Up the Mirmir
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party leaves Hamri, tracks Hiyasa upriver, follows the trail inland, reaches Yerkir-khor's monument, and stops just before the descent into the complex.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Ryu leads the party up the Mirmir and through the forest to Yerkir-khor's buried monument.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Leaving Hamri, the party climbs above the waterfall and follows the Mirmir inland through autumn forest, relying on Ryu's tracking and Blu's scouting to stay on Hiyasa's trail. They make camp after dark, resume the search the next day, find Hiyasa's abandoned campsite, and follow the cut trail farther from the river. After a day and a half of pursuit, they reach a massive stone flower-like monument inscribed in Terran as the resting place of Yerkir-khor the Undaunted, inspect the ropes and open trap door, and stop just before anyone begins the descent.

locationHints.context: tracking Hiyasa upriver and through the inland forest
locationHints.notes: The beat spans departure, overnight travel, the abandoned campsite, and surface inspection of the monument.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: The party follows the signs of her recent passage.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Scouts during the forest search.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: Named in the Terran inscription on the monument.

itemDetails.name: Terran monument inscription
itemDetails.notes: Identifies Yerkir-khor as the Undaunted Prince of Earth, Lord of the Rock Below, and Guardian of the Crystal Skies.

sourceShortSummaries: The party descends into the tomb, survives the first traps, and enters the crystal-mosaic halls.
The tomb's guardians awaken, forcing the party through a hard fight against stone gargoyles and a serpent statue.
sourceLongSummaries: Trok starts down the shaft, discovers carved handholds and a mosaic floor far below, and the party brings Nerissa's floating lights to see the crystal-cavern design. The descent goes wrong when a hidden blade trap knocks Trok down and Kaleho falls through the shaft, leaving the group split between the top and the hall below. After regrouping with ropes and caution, they study the glittering mosaic chamber, recognize more of the tomb's strange stonework, and reach the statue chamber just before the carved guardians fully become a fight.

The carved guardians animate into a fight with several winged stone gargoyles and a serpent-headed statue construct. Trok grows and smashes the small stone creatures, Nerissa's thunderous magic cracks their bodies, Kaleho shoots from range, and Ryu and Blu dive in to stop gargoyles that try to reach and activate the larger statue. When the last guardian shatters, the party leaves initiative and begins inspecting the broken defenders and the statue they had been trying to manipulate.

sourceEntries.title: Crystal Halls
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party descends into the shaft, triggers the first trap, studies the crystal-mosaic hall, and reaches the statue chamber before the first major fight fully begins.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party descends into the tomb, survives the first traps, and enters the crystal-mosaic halls.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Trok starts down the shaft, discovers carved handholds and a mosaic floor far below, and the party brings Nerissa's floating lights to see the crystal-cavern design. The descent goes wrong when a hidden blade trap knocks Trok down and Kaleho falls through the shaft, leaving the group split between the top and the hall below. After regrouping with ropes and caution, they study the glittering mosaic chamber, recognize more of the tomb's strange stonework, and reach the statue chamber just before the carved guardians fully become a fight.

locationHints.context: entrance shaft, crystal mosaic hall, and upper statue chamber
locationHints.notes: The party has fully entered the buried complex.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Remains with Ryu while the others begin the descent.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The party continues interpreting the tomb in light of the monument inscription.

itemDetails.name: crystal-cavern mosaic
itemDetails.notes: A multicolored floor mosaic below the shaft depicts glittering caves or gemstone caverns.

itemDetails.name: blade trap
itemDetails.notes: A hidden blade swings through the shaft during the descent.

sourceEntries.title: Gargoyle Ambush
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The chamber encounter resolves into a contained combat with gargoyles and a snake-headed statue, ending when the enemies are broken and examined.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The tomb's guardians awaken, forcing the party through a hard fight against stone gargoyles and a serpent statue.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The carved guardians animate into a fight with several winged stone gargoyles and a serpent-headed statue construct. Trok grows and smashes the small stone creatures, Nerissa's thunderous magic cracks their bodies, Kaleho shoots from range, and Ryu and Blu dive in to stop gargoyles that try to reach and activate the larger statue. When the last guardian shatters, the party leaves initiative and begins inspecting the broken defenders and the statue they had been trying to manipulate.

locationHints.context: guardian chamber near the upper halls
locationHints.notes: This chamber houses the first active defenders of the tomb.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Dive-bombs gargoyles during the fight.

itemDetails.name: loose amethyst gem
itemDetails.notes: Set in a gold band on the serpent statue and apparently targeted by the gargoyles.

mainEnemies.name: stone gargoyles
mainEnemies.notes: Winged stone guardians animated to defend the chamber.

mainEnemies.name: serpent statue construct
mainEnemies.notes: A snake-headed stone figure tied to the chamber's defenses.

combatHints.notes: The fight is contained in this beat and ends when the last active stone guardian is destroyed.

sourceShortSummaries: After the first fight, the party follows Hiyasa's tracks through locked doors, sticky mud, and lightning crystals toward the inner tomb.
The party finds Hiyasa trapped in stone beside the tomb's great guardian and fails to satisfy its demand for tribute.
When tribute fails, the guardian attacks and the party scrambles to survive the opening phase of the tomb battle.
The party begins to turn the fight by hammering the guardian with thunder, psychic force, weapons, and Blu's attacks.
The guardian turns the floor to mud and nearly entombs Trok, but Ryu kills it before the stone can close forever.
sourceLongSummaries: With the first guardians destroyed, the party finds stone keys on the shattered gargoyles and confirms that Hiyasa's trail leads through a locked door. They heal, mark hazards, and descend through mirror-image rooms and corridors while trying to preserve the tracks. The lower route forces them to solve or bypass a sticky mud-like floor, then crawl beneath a lightning-crystal trap around a Terran warning to the faithful of Yerkir-khor, bringing them to the chamber where Hiyasa is trapped.

In the inner chamber the party finally finds Hiyasa alive, dehydrated, and encased in stone from the upper thighs down. The room is covered in gem mosaics showing a stone-and-earth lord shaping valleys, flattening mountains, receiving offerings, and being honored by humans and dwarves. A large statue steps down from the tomb altar and demands tribute for Yerkir-khor, but the party's attempts to interpret the chamber and prepare offerings only bring them closer to confrontation.

The party offers shells, a pearl, and Kaleho's treasured merfolk book, but the guardian rejects each ocean-linked tribute as an insult to Yerkir-khor and grows angrier. Ryu throws a spear to start the fight, Blu enters the blue-lit room, and initiative begins. Kaleho opens from hiding, Nerissa uses sound magic and inspiration, Trok activates Eldritch Maul, Ryu and Blu strike hard, and Hiyasa warns that the guardian can turn stone to mud and back again.

As the battle grinds on, Nerissa moves away and keeps using piercing sound while the others try to keep pressure on the stone colossus. The guardian fails to laugh at Tasha's Hideous Laughter, retreats and reshapes stone around the chamber, and keeps forcing the party to reposition. Trok tries to close with Eldritch Maul and a water-tattoo charm, Ryu and Blu continue striking from range and above, and Kaleho looks for a way to keep sneak attack pressure on the guardian before the floor becomes the next crisis.

In the battle's most dangerous turn, the guardian liquefies the floor, restraining Nerissa and Hiyasa while a stone hand grabs Kaleho. It then knocks Trok unconscious, throws him into the mud, and leaves him sinking as the floor begins to harden back into stone. Ryu kills the guardian, revives Trok with a swarm of sardine spirits, and the party scrambles through the aftermath while Hiyasa frees herself, tries to pocket a jar, relaxes only after Trok invokes Orden, and begins sketching the map that led her to the tomb.

sourceEntries.title: Deeper into the Tomb
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the first fight, the party heals, loots, bypasses more traps, and follows muddy footprints toward the inner sanctum.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After the first fight, the party follows Hiyasa's tracks through locked doors, sticky mud, and lightning crystals toward the inner tomb.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With the first guardians destroyed, the party finds stone keys on the shattered gargoyles and confirms that Hiyasa's trail leads through a locked door. They heal, mark hazards, and descend through mirror-image rooms and corridors while trying to preserve the tracks. The lower route forces them to solve or bypass a sticky mud-like floor, then crawl beneath a lightning-crystal trap around a Terran warning to the faithful of Yerkir-khor, bringing them to the chamber where Hiyasa is trapped.

locationHints.context: lower corridors, locked rooms, mud trap, and lightning-crystal stair
locationHints.notes: The group advances steadily from the first guardian room toward the inner sanctum.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Her trail shows where she went after surviving earlier traps.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: Named in the warning that threatens the unfaithful with entombment.

itemDetails.name: stone keys
itemDetails.notes: Found around the broken gargoyles' necks and used to open the locked route.

itemDetails.name: lightning-crystal trap
itemDetails.notes: Fires from the railing crystals unless the party crawls beneath it.

sourceEntries.title: The Guardian's Terms
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reaches the inner tomb, finds Hiyasa trapped in stone, and unsuccessfully tries to satisfy the guardian's demand for tribute.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party finds Hiyasa trapped in stone beside the tomb's great guardian and fails to satisfy its demand for tribute.

The room is covered in gem mosaics showing a stone-and-earth lord shaping valleys, flattening mountains, receiving offerings, and being honored by humans and dwarves. A large statue steps down from the tomb altar and demands tribute for Yerkir-khor, but the party's attempts to interpret the chamber and prepare offerings only bring them closer to confrontation.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum before the central altar
locationHints.notes: The final chamber contains Hiyasa, tribute jars, murals, and the tomb's greatest guardian.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Alive but trapped in stone when the party finds her.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The chamber's inscriptions and guardian speak in his name.

itemDetails.name: gem-stoppered jars
itemDetails.notes: Six large jars stand behind the altar, each topped with a different gemstone.

itemDetails.name: inner sanctum mosaics
itemDetails.notes: Depict Yerkir-khor shaping earth, receiving offerings, and ruling over scenes of farming, mining, and trade.

sourceEntries.title: Guardian Battle Begins
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The guardian rejects the party's offerings and the first phase of the final battle establishes the enemy's stone-and-earth powers.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: When tribute fails, the guardian attacks and the party scrambles to survive the opening phase of the tomb battle.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party offers shells, a pearl, and Kaleho's treasured merfolk book, but the guardian rejects each ocean-linked tribute as an insult to Yerkir-khor and grows angrier. Ryu throws a spear to start the fight, Blu enters the blue-lit room, and initiative begins. Kaleho opens from hiding, Nerissa uses sound magic and inspiration, Trok activates Eldritch Maul, Ryu and Blu strike hard, and Hiyasa warns that the guardian can turn stone to mud and back again.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum around Hiyasa's stone prison
locationHints.notes: Combat begins in the same room where tribute negotiations failed.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Still trapped in stone while the fight begins around her.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Streaks down to attack the guardian during the opening rounds.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The guardian frames the rejected offerings as insults to Yerkir-khor.

itemDetails.name: ocean offerings
itemDetails.notes: Shells, a pearl, and a merfolk book are presented as tribute and rejected.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: The enormous construct that judges tribute in the inner tomb.

combatHints.notes: The opening rounds establish the guardian's earth-shaping powers and hostility to ocean-linked tribute.

sourceEntries.title: Cracking the Colossus
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The middle phase of the fight focuses on sustained damage, psychic and thunder effects, and the party's attempt to wear the guardian down.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party begins to turn the fight by hammering the guardian with thunder, psychic force, weapons, and Blu's attacks.
sourceEntries.longSummary: As the battle grinds on, Nerissa moves away and keeps using piercing sound while the others try to keep pressure on the stone colossus. The guardian fails to laugh at Tasha's Hideous Laughter, retreats and reshapes stone around the chamber, and keeps forcing the party to reposition. Trok tries to close with Eldritch Maul and a water-tattoo charm, Ryu and Blu continue striking from range and above, and Kaleho looks for a way to keep sneak attack pressure on the guardian before the floor becomes the next crisis.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum during the running fight with the guardian
locationHints.notes: The party is still engaged in continuous combat with the same foe.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Still stuck in stone while the party tries to win quickly.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Continues dive-bombing the guardian during the middle phase.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Still the only major enemy in the final chamber.

combatHints.notes: This phase centers on sustained damage, failed control magic, and positional pressure before the mud crisis.

sourceEntries.title: Mud and Rescue
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The guardian turns the floor to mud, nearly entombs Trok, and is finally destroyed when Ryu turns the battle.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The guardian turns the floor to mud and nearly entombs Trok, but Ryu kills it before the stone can close forever.
sourceEntries.longSummary: In the battle's most dangerous turn, the guardian liquefies the floor, restraining Nerissa and Hiyasa while a stone hand grabs Kaleho. It then knocks Trok unconscious, throws him into the mud, and leaves him sinking as the floor begins to harden back into stone. Ryu kills the guardian, revives Trok with a swarm of sardine spirits, and the party scrambles through the aftermath while Hiyasa frees herself, tries to pocket a jar, relaxes only after Trok invokes Orden, and begins sketching the map that led her to the tomb.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum as the stone floor turns to mud
locationHints.notes: The battlefield itself becomes the main hazard in the closing rounds.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Freed once the guardian is destroyed, then begins explaining what brought her to the tomb.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: Trok invokes Orden to convince Hiyasa the party came to rescue her.

itemDetails.name: gem-stoppered jars
itemDetails.notes: Hiyasa tries to slip one into her pack immediately after the fight.

itemDetails.name: Hiyasa's map sketch
itemDetails.notes: She begins drawing the map that marked three long-abandoned tombs.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Destroyed after nearly killing Trok by entombment.

combatHints.notes: Ryu's decisive attack ends the fight before the mud can harden around Trok.

sourceShortSummaries: With Hiyasa safe, the party loots the tomb, returns to Hamri, and identifies the strangest treasure from the jars.
sourceLongSummaries: After the guardian falls, Hiyasa explains that she thought this ancient tomb would be safer to rob than ancestral Mawar graves and admits she brought scrolls and a map from her years away. The party opens the gem-stoppered jars, finding archaic coins, rough gemstones, a pearl, yellow dust protecting ancient papers, red liquid that proves to be spoiled wine, and an obsidian jar with a one-way void that later shatters. They leave the tomb, disguise the entrance, return to Hamri a few days later, learn that the coins are ancient Orban, identify the pearl as a pearl of power, split about 600 gold of value, give Hiyasa a finder's fee, and leave the fragile unknown papers for later study.

sourceEntries.title: Return with the Spoils
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The final aftermath covers questioning Hiyasa, looting the tomb, and a later return to Hamri where the treasure and scrolls are divided.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: With Hiyasa safe, the party loots the tomb, returns to Hamri, and identifies the strangest treasure from the jars.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After the guardian falls, Hiyasa explains that she thought this ancient tomb would be safer to rob than ancestral Mawar graves and admits she brought scrolls and a map from her years away. The party opens the gem-stoppered jars, finding archaic coins, rough gemstones, a pearl, yellow dust protecting ancient papers, red liquid that proves to be spoiled wine, and an obsidian jar with a one-way void that later shatters. They leave the tomb, disguise the entrance, return to Hamri a few days later, learn that the coins are ancient Orban, identify the pearl as a pearl of power, split about 600 gold of value, give Hiyasa a finder's fee, and leave the fragile unknown papers for later study.

locationHints.context: tomb aftermath, return travel, and division of spoils at the inn
locationHints.notes: The beat spans the immediate aftermath in the tomb and the later return to town.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Returns with the party, accepts a finder's fee, and offers more information about the other map marks for payment.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: The party discusses whether to give money to him and Hiyasa.

itemDetails.name: Hiyasa's treasure map
itemDetails.notes: Shows three marked locations, including the tomb they just explored.

itemDetails.name: ancient Orban coins and gems
itemDetails.notes: Together worth about 600 gold after the return to Hamri.

itemDetails.name: pearl of power
itemDetails.notes: Recovered from the jars and eventually identified as a magic item that can restore a spell slot.

itemDetails.name: yellow dust and ancient papers
itemDetails.notes: The magical dust preserves a fragile sheaf of papers in an unknown language.

itemDetails.name: obsidian void jar
itemDetails.notes: Appears to contain a hole to nowhere but shatters after removal from the tomb.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The party continues interpreting the tomb in light of the monument inscription.

itemDetails.name: crystal-cavern mosaic
itemDetails.notes: A multicolored floor mosaic below the shaft depicts glittering caves or gemstone caverns.

itemDetails.name: blade trap
itemDetails.notes: A hidden blade swings through the shaft during the descent.

recapBlocks.title: Gargoyle Ambush
sourceShortSummaries: The tomb's guardians awaken, forcing the party through a hard fight against stone gargoyles and a serpent statue.
sourceLongSummaries: The carved guardians animate into a fight with several winged stone gargoyles and a serpent-headed statue construct. Trok grows and smashes the small stone creatures, Nerissa's thunderous magic cracks their bodies, Kaleho shoots from range, and Ryu and Blu dive in to stop gargoyles that try to reach and activate the larger statue. When the last guardian shatters, the party leaves initiative and begins inspecting the broken defenders and the statue they had been trying to manipulate.

sourceEntries.title: Gargoyle Ambush
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The chamber encounter resolves into a contained combat with gargoyles and a snake-headed statue, ending when the enemies are broken and examined.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The tomb's guardians awaken, forcing the party through a hard fight against stone gargoyles and a serpent statue.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The carved guardians animate into a fight with several winged stone gargoyles and a serpent-headed statue construct. Trok grows and smashes the small stone creatures, Nerissa's thunderous magic cracks their bodies, Kaleho shoots from range, and Ryu and Blu dive in to stop gargoyles that try to reach and activate the larger statue. When the last guardian shatters, the party leaves initiative and begins inspecting the broken defenders and the statue they had been trying to manipulate.

locationHints.context: guardian chamber near the upper halls
locationHints.notes: This chamber houses the first active defenders of the tomb.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Dive-bombs gargoyles during the fight.

itemDetails.name: loose amethyst gem
itemDetails.notes: Set in a gold band on the serpent statue and apparently targeted by the gargoyles.

mainEnemies.name: stone gargoyles
mainEnemies.notes: Winged stone guardians animated to defend the chamber.

mainEnemies.name: serpent statue construct
mainEnemies.notes: A snake-headed stone figure tied to the chamber's defenses.

combatHints.notes: The fight is contained in this beat and ends when the last active stone guardian is destroyed.

recapBlocks.title: Deeper into the Tomb
sourceShortSummaries: After the first fight, the party follows Hiyasa's tracks through locked doors, sticky mud, and lightning crystals toward the inner tomb.
sourceLongSummaries: With the first guardians destroyed, the party finds stone keys on the shattered gargoyles and confirms that Hiyasa's trail leads through a locked door. They heal, mark hazards, and descend through mirror-image rooms and corridors while trying to preserve the tracks. The lower route forces them to solve or bypass a sticky mud-like floor, then crawl beneath a lightning-crystal trap around a Terran warning to the faithful of Yerkir-khor, bringing them to the chamber where Hiyasa is trapped.

sourceEntries.title: Deeper into the Tomb
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the first fight, the party heals, loots, bypasses more traps, and follows muddy footprints toward the inner sanctum.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After the first fight, the party follows Hiyasa's tracks through locked doors, sticky mud, and lightning crystals toward the inner tomb.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With the first guardians destroyed, the party finds stone keys on the shattered gargoyles and confirms that Hiyasa's trail leads through a locked door. They heal, mark hazards, and descend through mirror-image rooms and corridors while trying to preserve the tracks. The lower route forces them to solve or bypass a sticky mud-like floor, then crawl beneath a lightning-crystal trap around a Terran warning to the faithful of Yerkir-khor, bringing them to the chamber where Hiyasa is trapped.

locationHints.context: lower corridors, locked rooms, mud trap, and lightning-crystal stair
locationHints.notes: The group advances steadily from the first guardian room toward the inner sanctum.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Her trail shows where she went after surviving earlier traps.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: Named in the warning that threatens the unfaithful with entombment.

itemDetails.name: stone keys
itemDetails.notes: Found around the broken gargoyles' necks and used to open the locked route.

itemDetails.name: lightning-crystal trap
itemDetails.notes: Fires from the railing crystals unless the party crawls beneath it.

recapBlocks.title: The Guardian's Terms
sourceShortSummaries: The party finds Hiyasa trapped in stone beside the tomb's great guardian and fails to satisfy its demand for tribute.
sourceLongSummaries: In the inner chamber the party finally finds Hiyasa alive, dehydrated, and encased in stone from the upper thighs down. The room is covered in gem mosaics showing a stone-and-earth lord shaping valleys, flattening mountains, receiving offerings, and being honored by humans and dwarves. A large statue steps down from the tomb altar and demands tribute for Yerkir-khor, but the party's attempts to interpret the chamber and prepare offerings only bring them closer to confrontation.

sourceEntries.title: The Guardian's Terms
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reaches the inner tomb, finds Hiyasa trapped in stone, and unsuccessfully tries to satisfy the guardian's demand for tribute.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party finds Hiyasa trapped in stone beside the tomb's great guardian and fails to satisfy its demand for tribute.
sourceEntries.longSummary: In the inner chamber the party finally finds Hiyasa alive, dehydrated, and encased in stone from the upper thighs down. The room is covered in gem mosaics showing a stone-and-earth lord shaping valleys, flattening mountains, receiving offerings, and being honored by humans and dwarves. A large statue steps down from the tomb altar and demands tribute for Yerkir-khor, but the party's attempts to interpret the chamber and prepare offerings only bring them closer to confrontation.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum before the central altar
locationHints.notes: The final chamber contains Hiyasa, tribute jars, murals, and the tomb's greatest guardian.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Alive but trapped in stone when the party finds her.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The chamber's inscriptions and guardian speak in his name.

itemDetails.name: gem-stoppered jars
itemDetails.notes: Six large jars stand behind the altar, each topped with a different gemstone.

itemDetails.name: inner sanctum mosaics
itemDetails.notes: Depict Yerkir-khor shaping earth, receiving offerings, and ruling over scenes of farming, mining, and trade.

recapBlocks.title: Guardian Battle Begins to Mud and Rescue
sourceShortSummaries: When tribute fails, the guardian attacks and the party scrambles to survive the opening phase of the tomb battle.
The party begins to turn the fight by hammering the guardian with thunder, psychic force, weapons, and Blu's attacks.
The guardian turns the floor to mud and nearly entombs Trok, but Ryu kills it before the stone can close forever.
sourceLongSummaries: The party offers shells, a pearl, and Kaleho's treasured merfolk book, but the guardian rejects each ocean-linked tribute as an insult to Yerkir-khor and grows angrier. Ryu throws a spear to start the fight, Blu enters the blue-lit room, and initiative begins. Kaleho opens from hiding, Nerissa uses sound magic and inspiration, Trok activates Eldritch Maul, Ryu and Blu strike hard, and Hiyasa warns that the guardian can turn stone to mud and back again.

As the battle grinds on, Nerissa moves away and keeps using piercing sound while the others try to keep pressure on the stone colossus. The guardian fails to laugh at Tasha's Hideous Laughter, retreats and reshapes stone around the chamber, and keeps forcing the party to reposition. Trok tries to close with Eldritch Maul and a water-tattoo charm, Ryu and Blu continue striking from range and above, and Kaleho looks for a way to keep sneak attack pressure on the guardian before the floor becomes the next crisis.

In the battle's most dangerous turn, the guardian liquefies the floor, restraining Nerissa and Hiyasa while a stone hand grabs Kaleho. It then knocks Trok unconscious, throws him into the mud, and leaves him sinking as the floor begins to harden back into stone. Ryu kills the guardian, revives Trok with a swarm of sardine spirits, and the party scrambles through the aftermath while Hiyasa frees herself, tries to pocket a jar, relaxes only after Trok invokes Orden, and begins sketching the map that led her to the tomb.

sourceEntries.title: Guardian Battle Begins
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The guardian rejects the party's offerings and the first phase of the final battle establishes the enemy's stone-and-earth powers.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: When tribute fails, the guardian attacks and the party scrambles to survive the opening phase of the tomb battle.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party offers shells, a pearl, and Kaleho's treasured merfolk book, but the guardian rejects each ocean-linked tribute as an insult to Yerkir-khor and grows angrier. Ryu throws a spear to start the fight, Blu enters the blue-lit room, and initiative begins. Kaleho opens from hiding, Nerissa uses sound magic and inspiration, Trok activates Eldritch Maul, Ryu and Blu strike hard, and Hiyasa warns that the guardian can turn stone to mud and back again.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum around Hiyasa's stone prison
locationHints.notes: Combat begins in the same room where tribute negotiations failed.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Still trapped in stone while the fight begins around her.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Streaks down to attack the guardian during the opening rounds.

npcDetails.name: Yerkir-khor
npcDetails.notes: The guardian frames the rejected offerings as insults to Yerkir-khor.

itemDetails.name: ocean offerings
itemDetails.notes: Shells, a pearl, and a merfolk book are presented as tribute and rejected.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: The enormous construct that judges tribute in the inner tomb.

combatHints.notes: The opening rounds establish the guardian's earth-shaping powers and hostility to ocean-linked tribute.

sourceEntries.title: Cracking the Colossus
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The middle phase of the fight focuses on sustained damage, psychic and thunder effects, and the party's attempt to wear the guardian down.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party begins to turn the fight by hammering the guardian with thunder, psychic force, weapons, and Blu's attacks.
sourceEntries.longSummary: As the battle grinds on, Nerissa moves away and keeps using piercing sound while the others try to keep pressure on the stone colossus. The guardian fails to laugh at Tasha's Hideous Laughter, retreats and reshapes stone around the chamber, and keeps forcing the party to reposition. Trok tries to close with Eldritch Maul and a water-tattoo charm, Ryu and Blu continue striking from range and above, and Kaleho looks for a way to keep sneak attack pressure on the guardian before the floor becomes the next crisis.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum during the running fight with the guardian
locationHints.notes: The party is still engaged in continuous combat with the same foe.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Still stuck in stone while the party tries to win quickly.

npcDetails.name: Blu
npcDetails.notes: Continues dive-bombing the guardian during the middle phase.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Still the only major enemy in the final chamber.

combatHints.notes: This phase centers on sustained damage, failed control magic, and positional pressure before the mud crisis.

sourceEntries.title: Mud and Rescue
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The guardian turns the floor to mud, nearly entombs Trok, and is finally destroyed when Ryu turns the battle.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The guardian turns the floor to mud and nearly entombs Trok, but Ryu kills it before the stone can close forever.
sourceEntries.longSummary: In the battle's most dangerous turn, the guardian liquefies the floor, restraining Nerissa and Hiyasa while a stone hand grabs Kaleho. It then knocks Trok unconscious, throws him into the mud, and leaves him sinking as the floor begins to harden back into stone. Ryu kills the guardian, revives Trok with a swarm of sardine spirits, and the party scrambles through the aftermath while Hiyasa frees herself, tries to pocket a jar, relaxes only after Trok invokes Orden, and begins sketching the map that led her to the tomb.

locationHints.context: inner sanctum as the stone floor turns to mud
locationHints.notes: The battlefield itself becomes the main hazard in the closing rounds.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Freed once the guardian is destroyed, then begins explaining what brought her to the tomb.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: Trok invokes Orden to convince Hiyasa the party came to rescue her.

itemDetails.name: gem-stoppered jars
itemDetails.notes: Hiyasa tries to slip one into her pack immediately after the fight.

itemDetails.name: Hiyasa's map sketch
itemDetails.notes: She begins drawing the map that marked three long-abandoned tombs.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Destroyed after nearly killing Trok by entombment.

combatHints.notes: Ryu's decisive attack ends the fight before the mud can harden around Trok.

recapBlocks.title: Return with the Spoils
sourceShortSummaries: With Hiyasa safe, the party loots the tomb, returns to Hamri, and identifies the strangest treasure from the jars.
sourceLongSummaries: After the guardian falls, Hiyasa explains that she thought this ancient tomb would be safer to rob than ancestral Mawar graves and admits she brought scrolls and a map from her years away. The party opens the gem-stoppered jars, finding archaic coins, rough gemstones, a pearl, yellow dust protecting ancient papers, red liquid that proves to be spoiled wine, and an obsidian jar with a one-way void that later shatters. They leave the tomb, disguise the entrance, return to Hamri a few days later, learn that the coins are ancient Orban, identify the pearl as a pearl of power, split about 600 gold of value, give Hiyasa a finder's fee, and leave the fragile unknown papers for later study.

sourceEntries.title: Return with the Spoils
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The final aftermath covers questioning Hiyasa, looting the tomb, and a later return to Hamri where the treasure and scrolls are divided.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: With Hiyasa safe, the party loots the tomb, returns to Hamri, and identifies the strangest treasure from the jars.

The party opens the gem-stoppered jars, finding archaic coins, rough gemstones, a pearl, yellow dust protecting ancient papers, red liquid that proves to be spoiled wine, and an obsidian jar with a one-way void that later shatters. They leave the tomb, disguise the entrance, return to Hamri a few days later, learn that the coins are ancient Orban, identify the pearl as a pearl of power, split about 600 gold of value, give Hiyasa a finder's fee, and leave the fragile unknown papers for later study.

locationHints.context: tomb aftermath, return travel, and division of spoils at the inn
locationHints.notes: The beat spans the immediate aftermath in the tomb and the later return to town.

npcDetails.name: Hiyasa
npcDetails.notes: Returns with the party, accepts a finder's fee, and offers more information about the other map marks for payment.

npcDetails.name: Orden
npcDetails.notes: The party discusses whether to give money to him and Hiyasa.

itemDetails.name: Hiyasa's treasure map
itemDetails.notes: Shows three marked locations, including the tomb they just explored.

itemDetails.name: ancient Orban coins and gems
itemDetails.notes: Together worth about 600 gold after the return to Hamri.

itemDetails.name: pearl of power
itemDetails.notes: Recovered from the jars and eventually identified as a magic item that can restore a spell slot.

itemDetails.name: yellow dust and ancient papers
itemDetails.notes: The magical dust preserves a fragile sheaf of papers in an unknown language.

itemDetails.name: obsidian void jar
itemDetails.notes: Appears to contain a hole to nowhere but shatters after removal from the tomb.

locations.name: Hamri
locations.contextHint: Trok's shack on the beach below the towers

locations.name: Yerkir-khor monument
locations.contextHint: tracking Hiyasa upriver and through the inland forest
